title: Academic: hypothesis
question: A ___ is a proposed explanation that can be tested through research.
explanation: A hypothesis is a tentative explanation at the start of research, before evidence is gathered. A conclusion comes at the end.

title: Academic: methodology
question: The ___ section of a research paper describes how the study was conducted.
explanation: Methodology refers to the system of methods and procedures used in research. It explains the 'how' of the study.

title: Academic: significant
question: The results were statistically ___, meaning they were unlikely to have occurred by chance.
explanation: Statistically significant means the results are meaningful and not due to random variation.

title: Academic: analysis
question: After collecting the data, the researchers performed a detailed ___.
explanation: Analysis is the systematic examination of data to draw conclusions. It comes after data collection.

title: Academic: empirical
question: ___ evidence is based on observation and experiment rather than theory alone.
explanation: Empirical evidence comes from direct observation or experimentation, as opposed to purely theoretical reasoning.

title: Academic: correlation
question: The study found a strong ___ between exercise frequency and sleep quality.
explanation: A correlation is a statistical relationship between two variables. It does not necessarily imply causation.

title: Academic: peer review
question: Before publication, the paper underwent ___, where other experts evaluated it.
explanation: Peer review is the process where qualified experts in the same field evaluate research before it is published.

title: Academic: abstract
question: The ___ provides a brief summary of the entire research paper.
explanation: An abstract is a concise summary at the beginning of a paper that covers the purpose, methods, results, and conclusions.

title: Academic: qualitative
question: ___ research focuses on understanding experiences and meanings rather than measuring numbers.
explanation: Qualitative research explores qualities, descriptions, and meanings. Quantitative research deals with numerical data.

title: Academic: citation
question: You must include a ___ when you refer to another author's work in your paper.
explanation: A citation is a formal reference to a source, giving credit to the original author and allowing readers to find the source.

title: Professional: revenue
question: The company's annual ___, meaning total income from sales, exceeded ten million dollars last year.
explanation: Revenue is the total income generated by a business from its operations, before any costs are subtracted. Expenditure is money spent, a deficit is a shortfall, and a liability is a debt.

title: Professional: stakeholder
question: Every ___ in the project, from investors to end users, was consulted before the launch.
explanation: A stakeholder is anyone with an interest in or affected by a project. Shareholders specifically own company shares.

title: Professional: deadline
question: We need to submit the proposal before the ___ on Friday.
explanation: A deadline is the latest time by which something must be completed. A milestone is a significant point of progress.

title: Professional: negotiate
question: The two companies had to ___ the terms of the contract for several weeks.
explanation: To negotiate means to discuss terms in order to reach an agreement. It involves back-and-forth discussion between parties.

title: Professional: implement
question: The team will ___ the new software system across all departments next quarter.
explanation: To implement means to put a plan or decision into effect. It implies carrying out something that has already been planned.

title: Professional: delegate
question: A good manager knows how to ___ tasks to the right team members.
explanation: To delegate means to assign responsibility or authority to someone else. It specifically refers to tasks and decision-making.

title: Professional: feasible
question: After reviewing the engineering constraints, the board decided the expansion plan was not ___.
explanation: Feasible means possible and practical to achieve. Given engineering constraints, the question is whether the plan can actually be carried out.

title: Professional: agenda
question: Please review the ___ before the meeting so you know which topics will be discussed.
explanation: An agenda is a list of items to be discussed at a meeting. Minutes are the written record of what was discussed after the meeting.

title: Professional: quarterly
question: The company publishes ___ reports to update investors every three months.
explanation: Quarterly means occurring every three months, or four times per year. Annual means once per year, biweekly every two weeks, and monthly every month.

title: Professional: liability
question: The insurance covers the company against any legal ___ from product defects.
explanation: A liability is a legal responsibility or obligation, especially financial. In this context, it means potential legal costs.

title: Formal: furthermore
question: Which discourse marker adds an additional supporting point (meaning 'in addition, moreover')?
explanation: Furthermore adds supporting information. Nevertheless signals contrast ('despite that'), Otherwise signals an alternative condition ('if not'), and Conversely signals an opposite view — none of them add a supporting point.

title: Formal: consequently
question: Which discourse marker introduces a direct cause-and-effect result (meaning 'as a result, therefore')?
explanation: Consequently introduces a result. Conversely signals an opposite view, Nevertheless signals contrast despite something, and Alternatively signals a different option — none of them express cause and effect.

title: Formal: regarding
question: I am writing to you ___ the position advertised on your website.
explanation: Regarding means 'about' or 'concerning.' It is used in formal writing to introduce the topic of discussion.

title: Formal: notwithstanding
question: Which formal word means 'in spite of' or 'despite' and signals that an obstacle did not prevent the outcome?
explanation: Notwithstanding means 'in spite of' or 'despite.' Furthermore adds a point, Meanwhile signals simultaneous time, and Henceforth means 'from now on' — none of them express 'despite.'

title: Formal: whereas
question: The first method is fast, ___ the second is more accurate.
explanation: Whereas introduces a contrast between two facts or situations. It is more formal than 'while' or 'but.'

title: Formal: hereby
question: We ___ confirm your appointment as Regional Director, effective immediately.
explanation: Hereby means 'by this means' or 'as a result of this document or statement.' It is used in official declarations.

title: Formal: in lieu of
question: Which formal phrase means 'instead of' or 'in place of'?
explanation: In lieu of means 'instead of.' In exchange of is ungrammatical (the correct phrase is 'in exchange for'), in advance of means 'before,' and in charge of means 'responsible for' — none of them express substitution.

title: Formal: pursuant to
question: Which formal phrase means 'in accordance with' and is used to reference a rule or agreement being followed?
explanation: Pursuant to means 'in accordance with.' The other three are all ungrammatical: the correct forms are 'pursuant to,' 'according to,' and 'in accordance with' — 'pursuant of,' 'according with,' and 'accordant to' do not exist in standard English.

title: Formal: henceforth
question: Which formal adverb means 'from this point on' (referring to time moving forward from now)?
explanation: Henceforth means 'from this time on.' Heretofore means the opposite ('before now'), and 'hithertofore' and 'thencefrom' are not standard English words.

title: Formal: albeit
question: The results were positive, ___ somewhat unexpected.
explanation: Albeit means 'although' or 'even though.' It introduces a qualification or slight contrast to the main statement.

title: Presentation: outline
question: In today's presentation, I'd like to ___ three key strategies for growth.
explanation: To outline means to describe the main features or points of something. In presentations, it means giving a summary of what will be covered.

title: Presentation: elaborate
question: Could you ___ on that point? I'd like to understand it in more detail.
explanation: To elaborate means to explain something in more detail. It is commonly used in meetings and Q&A sessions.

title: Presentation: recap
question: Let me quickly ___ the main points before we move on.
explanation: To recap means to summarize or go over the key points again. Retract means to withdraw a statement, which is very different.

title: Presentation: convey
question: The chart is designed to ___ the relationship between cost and performance.
explanation: To convey means to communicate or express information. A chart is meant to make information clear, not hide it.

title: Presentation: transition
question: Now I'd like to make a ___ to our second topic, market expansion.
explanation: A transition is a smooth change from one topic or section to another. It is essential for well-structured presentations.

title: Meeting: minutes
question: The chairperson asked the secretary to record the official ___ of the board meeting.
explanation: Minutes are the official written record of what is discussed and decided in a meeting. They follow a formal structure required for governance.

title: Meeting: adjourn
question: If there are no further questions, I suggest we ___ the meeting, as is customary after all items on the agenda are resolved.
explanation: To adjourn means to formally end or suspend a meeting as part of normal procedure. Abolish is used for laws and institutions; admonish and acquit apply to people, not meetings.

title: Meeting: consensus
question: After a long discussion, the committee finally reached a ___, with all members in full agreement.
explanation: A consensus is a general agreement among all members. A compromise involves each side giving something up, which is different.

title: Meeting: table (verb)
question: The board decided to ___ the discussion until more data was available.
explanation: To table a discussion means to postpone or defer it to a later time. It does not mean to cancel or end it permanently.

title: Meeting: motion
question: A ___ was put forward to approve the new budget proposal.
explanation: A motion is a formal proposal made during a meeting for the group to vote on. It is standard parliamentary procedure.

title: Report: findings
question: The ___ of the investigation were published in the final report.
explanation: Findings are the results or conclusions discovered through research or investigation. They are based on evidence, not opinion.

title: Report: comprehensive
question: The report provides a ___ overview of all aspects of the project.
explanation: Comprehensive means complete and including all relevant details. It implies thorough coverage of the subject.

title: Report: preliminary
question: The ___ results suggest the treatment is effective, but further testing is needed.
explanation: Preliminary means early-stage or initial. It implies the results are not yet final and more work is required.

title: Report: benchmark
question: The industry ___ for customer satisfaction is 85%, and our company scored 91%.
explanation: A benchmark is a standard or reference point against which things can be compared or measured.

title: Report: appendix
question: Additional data tables can be found in the ___ at the end of the report.
explanation: An appendix contains supplementary material at the end of a document. A preface and foreword appear at the beginning.

title: Report: executive summary
question: The ___ gives senior managers a brief overview without requiring them to read the full document.
explanation: An executive summary is a short section at the start of a report that highlights the key points for busy decision-makers.

title: Professional: scalable
question: We need a ___ solution that can grow as the number of users increases.
explanation: Scalable means able to increase in size or capacity without losing performance. Stagnant, static, and stable all imply no growth, which contradicts the requirement.

title: Professional: streamline
question: The new software will ___ the approval process, reducing it from five steps to two.
explanation: To streamline means to make a process more efficient by simplifying or eliminating unnecessary steps.

title: Professional: compliance
question: All departments must ensure ___ with the new data protection regulations.
explanation: Compliance means acting in accordance with rules, laws, or regulations. It is about following established requirements.

title: Academic: plagiarism
question: Submitting someone else's work as your own is considered ___.
explanation: Plagiarism is the act of using another person's work or ideas without proper attribution and presenting them as your own.
